original: Harriet Tubmanarrives in Auburn, New York, on her last mission to free slaves, having evaded capture for eight years on the Underground Railroad
Dutch Resistance fighter Corrie ten Boom is accidentally released from the Ravensbrück concentration camp, living to speak and educate of her experience in "The Hiding Place"
"The Chipmunk Song" by the Chipmunks (Alvin, Simon & Theodore with David Seville) hits #1
[Grace] Nichelle Nichols, American actress (Star Trek - "Lt. Uhura") and singer (Down To Earth; "Fly Me To The Moon"), born in Robbins, Illinois (d. 2022)
American inventor Josephine Cochrane is granted a patent for the dishwasher
First observation of Neptune -Galileoobserves and records a "fixed star" without realizing it is a planet

"The Greatest Game Ever Played": Baltimore Colts win the 26th NFL championship against the New York Giants 23-17 at Yankee Stadium, in the first sudden-death overtime game in NFL history. 17 future members of the Pro Football Hall of Fame are involved in the game.
US President Nixon signs Endangered Species Act into law
Creosote bush determined to be 11,700 years old
Filmmakers the Lumière brothers hold the first commercial film screening at the Salon Indien du Grand Café in Paris
1st American test-tube baby, Elizabeth Jordan Carr is born in Norfolk, Virginia
Montreal right wing Maurice 'Rocket' Richard becomes first player in NHL history to score 8 points in one game with 5 goals and 3 assists in the Canadiens' 9-1 win over the Detroit Red Wings

"The Hail Mary", with 32 seconds left in the NFC Divisional Playoff Game, Cowboys quarterback Roger Staubach throws a legendary 50-yard winning touchdown pass to Drew Pearson to defeat the Minnesota Vikings, and the "Hail Mary" play gains national recognition
Film "Bright Eyes" premieres starringShirley Templeand featuring the song "On the Good Ship Lollipop"
William Finley Semple of Mount Vernon, Ohio, patents chewing gum

UFC bantamweight champion Amanda Nunes of Brazil moves up in weight and KOs dominant featherweight Cris "Cyborg" Justino just 51 seconds into the opening round at the Forum in Inglewood, CA; first female to win UFC titles in multiple weight classes
Sting defeats Hollywood Hogan for the WCW Championship
The Beatles' "The Beatles" aka "The White Album" goes #1 & stays #1 for 9 weeks
US announced a study to launch an Earth satellite
North British Railway's train falls as Tay bridge collapses (Scotland)
Comet Kohoutek at perihelion
Kary B. Mullis, American biochemist and winner of Nobel Prize in Chemistry 1993, born in Lenoir, North Carolina (d. 2019)[1]

Ballon d'Or: Ajax forwardJohan Cruyffwins award for best European football player ahead of Inter forward Sandro Mazzola and Manchester United winger George Best; first Dutch national and Ajax player to win award
Ballon d'Or: Juventus striker Paolo Rossi is named best European football player; beats Bordeaux midfielder Alain Giresse and Juventus' Polish midfielder Zbigniew Boniek
Ballon d'Or: Benfica striker Eusébio wins award for best European football player; first Portuguese national to win, and still only Benfica player
Scottish Lotus driverJim Clarkwins his record 7th Grand Prix of the F1 season, the South African event at Prince George Circuit; first Scotsman to win the World Drivers Championship
Test cricket debutant Scott Boland takes 6 for 7 as Australia retain the Ashes (3-0) with an innings & 14 run win against England in the 3rd Test in Melbourne
First ever Women's cricket international begins England vs Australia in Australia
In the aftermath of the Cold War, the New York Rangers become first team in NHL history to face a touring Soviet hockey squad; Soviet Army beats the Rangers, 7-3 at Madison Square Garden
100,000 attend Miami Pop Festival in Florida; performers included: TheJimi HendrixExperience; The Mothers of Invention;Chuck Berry; John Lee Hooker; The Crazy World of Arthur Brown; and Blues Image
Leonard Bernstein,Betty Comden, andAdolph Green's musical "On the Town" with choreography byJerome Robbins, opens at the Adelphi Theatre, NYC; runs for 462 performances
Arthur Eddington, English astrophysicist, cosmologist and mathematician (stars), born in Kendal, Westmorland, England (d. 1944)
Maarten Schmidt, Dutch-American astronomer who first identified a quasar, born in Groningen, Netherlands (d. 2022)
Trans-Pacific cable links Hawaii to US
United States claims Midway Island, the first territory annexed outside continental limits
Chris Weidman defeats Anderson Silva to retain the UFC Middleweight Championship
Blockbuster Bowl 1: Florida State beats Penn State, 24-17
Montreal'sGeorges Vezinabecomes the first NHL goalie to record an assist during the Canadiens’ 6-3 win over the Toronto Arenas
Gary Cosier scores 109 v West Indies at MCG on Test Cricket debut
India all out for 66 at Durban after making 100 in cricket 1st innings
Andrea Bocellimakes his classical debut singing at a concert at Teatro Romolo Valli in Reggio Emilia, Italy
Ma Rainey, "Mother of the Blues" makes her last record - "Big Feelin' Blues"

Johnny Otis [Ioannis Veliotes], American R&B musician, songwriter, producer and talent scout ("Willie and the Hand Jive"; "Cupid's Boogie"), known as "the godfather of rhythm and blues", born in Vallejo California (d. 2012)
Earl "Fatha" Hines, American jazz pianist and bandleader (Deep Forest), born in Duquesne, Pennsylvania (d. 1983)
Martha Wash, American disco singer-songwriter (Two Tons O' Fun; The Weather Girls - "It's Raining Men"), born in San Francisco, California
Alex Chilton, American rock guitarist, vocalist, songwriter, and producer (Box Tops - "The Letter"; Big Star - "Thirteen"), born in Memphis, Tennessee (d. 2010)
Edgar Winter, American rock musician ("Frankenstein"; "Free Ride"), born in Beaumont, Texas
Nigel Kennedy, British classical, jazz and klezmer violinist, born in Brighton, England
Joan Anderson, Australian-American model credited with bringing the Hula-Hoop to America, born in Sydney, Australia (d. 2025)[1]
Roebuck "Pops" Staples, American gospel singer (The Staple Singers), born in Winona, Mississippi (d. 2000)
(Joseph) "Ziggy" Modeliste American R&B and funk-rock drummer (The Meters; The New Barbarians), also known as 'Zigaboo', born in New Orleans, Louisiana
Charles Neville, American vocalist and saxophonist (Neville Brothers), born in New Orleans, Louisiana (d. 2018)
Kenneth Gant, American funk bassist and vocalist (Midnight Star - "No Parking On The Dance Floor"; "Operator"), record producer, and real estate agent, born in the USA
Earthquake at Newcastle Australia, 11 die
Sperm whale beaches at Noordwijk, Netherlands
1st daily wireless weather forecasts published (London)
1st municipally owned streetcars take to the streets in San Francisco, California as SF Municipal Railway starts operation at Geary St (MUNI)
Pravda publishes a letter by Pavel Postyshev, who revives New Year tree tradition in the Soviet Union
The DC-3 airliner NC16002 disappears 50 miles south of Miami, Florida.
Japanese Emperor Hanazono ascends to the throne at age 11
